Home How it works Why Velvoix Pilot Contact

Velvoix

From signal to clear care action

Velvoix helps healthcare organisations turn button calls, voice input and other care-related signals into explainable, prioritised and auditable care workflow.

Explore Velvoix See how it works

Built for care environments where clarity, follow-up and reconstruction matter.

The challenge

When a signal is not yet a clear care action

Many systems can register that something happened, but they do not sufficiently support the next step: what is likely going on, how urgent is it, what follow-up fits the situation and how can the full sequence later be reconstructed properly?

Explainability

Why

Help button was triggered repeatedly within a short interval.

Based on triage rules and event context.

Primary signal

Repeated help request

Core observation

Follow-up is likely needed again.

Triage basis

Repeat trigger in a short interval and contextual signal data.

Operational follow-up

Recommended action

Go directly to the resident; check the situation.

Urgency

Direct

SLA status

SLA critical

Owner

Not yet assigned

Chain difference

Not from alert to interpretation, but from signal to structured workflow

The difference sits in the chain: where traditional systems stop at an alert, Velvoix adds context, triage and event formation so follow-up becomes manageable.

Comparison

Traditional chain

Signal
→
Alert
→
Manual interpretation

Velvoix chain

Each extra layer adds meaning and makes follow-up, escalation and audit manageable.

Signal

A button call, voice input or other care-related signal enters in a controlled way as the starting point for follow-up.

Less interpretation burden
Better prioritisation
Better reconstruction

How it works

From signal to workable event outcome

Velvoix does not just register signals, but adds context, triage and actionable event flow so care teams understand faster what needs attention and which follow-up makes sense.

Understand faster what needs attention
Recommended action becomes workable immediately
Audit and reconstruction remain intact

Event assembly

From raw input to a workable event with context, recommended action, status and audit.

Input received

Signal received

A button call, voice input or other care-related signal is received in a controlled way.

Context added

Context added

Repeat behaviour, history and situational context are added immediately.

Triage structured

Triage structured

Urgency and recommended action become visible in explainable care language.

Urgency Direct
Why Repeated within a short interval
Action Go directly to the resident; check the situation

Operational event created

Operational event created

Status, ownership and team follow-up become directly manageable.

Status Open
Ownership Not yet assigned
Follow-up Workable for team handling

Audit attached

Audit attached

Status flow and reconstruction remain traceable for evaluation and audit.

Why Velvoix is different

Why Velvoix works differently in operations

Velvoix is not built to pass signals through, but to structure care situations into explainable, manageable and auditable workflow.

01

Not a notification layer

Velvoix does not pass signals through as isolated alerts, but turns them into workable events with context, ownership and follow-up.

Less interpretation burden, tighter follow-up.

02

Explainable triage

Velvoix shows not only urgency, but also why something needs attention and which follow-up makes sense in care language.

Better prioritisation, more understandable action.

03

Event truth for workflow and audit

Status, ownership, escalation and reconstruction all build on one operational truth.

Consistent workflow, traceable reconstruction.

Technical core

Built for reliable follow-up under pressure

The strength of Velvoix does not sit in a standalone screen, model or signal source, but in a product core that keeps event truth, explainable triage, follow-up and governance together in a controlled way, even when operations are under pressure or parts of the chain are temporarily limited.

Backend truth
Honest fallback
Auditable follow-up
Role-bound access
Tenant separation
Chain observability

01

Remains usable under partial failure

Velvoix is designed so follow-up can remain usable when parts of the chain are temporarily limited. Not by pretending certainty, but by keeping core status and next action visible in a controlled way.

Reliable workflow when not every part of the chain reacts perfectly.

02

No false certainty

If context, quality or availability is limited, that should remain visible and manageable instead of disappearing behind apparently certain output.

Honest fallback instead of silent degradation.

03

Backend truth at the core

The operational truth about status, explanation and follow-up is held in the core, not spread across screen logic or derived integration outcomes.

Backend-owned truth over fragmented UI or integration logic.

04

Role- and tenant-bound

Access and actions remain bounded by organisation, context and role. That keeps Velvoix governable when multiple teams and environments operate side by side.

Hard boundaries between organisations, roles and contexts.

05

Reconstructable and auditable

Follow-up, status changes and critical decisions remain traceable, so evaluation and quality improvement do not depend on memory or disconnected exports.

Traceable follow-up instead of isolated snapshots.

06

Operationally manageable

Observability, ownership and governance are part of product behaviour. That keeps it visible where attention is needed and how teams should respond in a controlled way.

Operational control as product discipline, not an IT afterthought.

Together, these layers keep Velvoix from becoming a notification layer or black-box layer and make it an explainable, governable care platform.

Pilot and partnership

Explore whether a pilot fits

A pilot helps make clear how Velvoix fits existing care practice, team routines and operational processes.

Suitable for nursing homes, long-term care, operational care teams and strategic collaboration partners.

Schedule a pilot conversation Discuss collaboration
Pilot and partnership

In a pilot we explore

01

Operational clarity

See faster what needs attention, why that is the case and which follow-up makes sense.

02

Workable follow-up

Organise ownership, status and next action more clearly inside the daily care workflow.

03

Reconstruction and evaluation

Make status flow, escalation and retrospective visibility usable for quality improvement.

04

Fit and integration

Explore how Velvoix connects to teams, processes, devices and the existing environment.

For healthcare organisations
Focus on workload, follow-up, clarity and reconstruction.
For partners
Focus on fit, operational value and strengthening the care chain.
